content.problemStatement:
Write a program using a **for** loop to find and print the index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in any given list of NNN integers.

Check the sample input / output below for further details.

Note that your output needs to take into account that the array is 000 indexed.


Input Format

First line of input contains a positive integer NNN - the length of the input array.
Second line of input contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output a single integer - the index (0 - based) of the first occurrence of the number 8.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer N, the length of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer – the 0‑based index of the first occurrence of the number 8 in the given array, or -1 if 8 does not appear.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first element is 8, so the answer is index 0.
There is no 8 in the array, thus we output -1.

solution.approach: We scan the array from left to right keeping the index of the first element equal to 8. As soon as we encounter 8 we store its index and ignore the rest of the elements. If the scan finishes without finding 8 we output -1. This single pass is optimal because any solution must look at each element until the first 8 is found.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int n; if(scanf("%d",&n)!=1) return 0; int ans=-1; for(int i=0;i<n;++i){int x; scanf("%d",&x); if(ans==-1 && x==8) ans=i;} printf("%d\n",ans); return 0; }
